evidenceKind: reviewed deterministic local NumPy teaching fixture

starter.expected: All thirteen watchdog, precedence, fallback-ownership, rollback, monotonic freshness-window, current-receipt, assistance, and authority-transfer checks are true.
starter.boundary: This command executes deterministic local authority and rollback logic; it does not measure physical stop distance, human factors, or operational safety.

requiredComponents: nominal controller and independent stale-camera watchdog
typed camera-health packet with monotonic capture/receive times, a bounded freshness window, and a consecutive-fresh gate
controller, mode, counter, and RNG snapshot/rollback
explicit human resume receipt tied to current, unexpired recovery evidence
buildOrder: Install the pinned NumPy dependency and run tests/test_watchdog.py, tests/test_authority.py, and tests/test_rollback.py
Freeze camera age, validity, freshness, authority, stop, fallback, and resume contracts
Prove watchdog-stop precedence against policy, fallback, and human motion requests
Inject the stale-camera fault and record requested-to-applied containment
Corrupt then restore the complete paired controller/RNG snapshot
Require three checked consecutive fresh packets inside the declared temporal window and a current, unexpired human receipt before assisted resume

acceptanceChecks.action: Request a policy move while an 80 ms stale camera packet activates the independent watchdog and competing authority requests are present.
acceptanceChecks.expected: Watchdog stop wins non-overridable precedence, requested 0.08 m becomes applied 0.0 m, and the controller position remains unchanged.

acceptanceChecks.action: Corrupt controller state after the fault, restore the accepted snapshot, then feed valid packets plus reversed capture/receive times, an overlong recovery window, and a bad packet between good packets.
acceptanceChecks.expected: Controller and RNG hash return exactly; fallback owns every decision; reversed time, overlong windows, and bad packets reset progress; and readiness occurs only after three new consecutive valid packets.

acceptanceChecks.action: Attempt resume with predated and expired receipts before presenting a current receipt with operator, reason, evidence time, timestamp, prior mode, selected owner, and applied action.
acceptanceChecks.expected: Predated and expired receipts are rejected; human authority owns only the current unexpired resume; the action remains a hold; and the completed path is labeled assisted rather than autonomous.

manifest.matrixIdentity: rowIndex identifies the ordered executable authority events; phase is the emitted event phase.

resumeTiming.predatedError: resume receipt cannot predate current camera evidence
resumeTiming.expiredError: current camera evidence expired before resume

decision: The independent watchdog wins authority and holds the faulted command; rollback restores the paired state, fallback owns bounded monotonic freshness checks, and only a current unexpired human receipt resumes control.
failure: The stale-camera detect row, corrupted rollback hash, temporal-window faults, and invalid receipt attempts remain explicit; any policy override, broken freshness sequence, or stale/predated resume fails acceptance.
boundary: This is real local deterministic authority and rollback logic. It is not a learned-policy result from a robot, a physical stop-distance measurement, a human-factors study, or an operational safety certification.
